arXivLabs is a framework that allows collaborators to develop and share new arXiv features directly on our website.

URL: https://res.cloudinary.com/icecube/images/q_auto/w_788,h_604/v1608055769/news_attachment.file_.bc7ab9c2304e1e91.53637265656e2053686f7420323031352d31302d323820617420392e30382e343620414d2e706e67/news_attachment.file_.bc7ab9c2304e1e91.53637265656e2053686f7420323031352d31302d323820617420392e30382e343620414d2e706e67.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the process of detecting solar neutrinos at the Sudbury Neutrino Observatory (SNO) in Ontario, Canada, showing interactions with heavy water and highlighting the production of different neutrino types and their detection via Cherenkov radiation.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://slideplayer.com/slide/13364105/80/images/7/In+2002,+the+SNO+results+confirmed+the+hypothesis+of+neutrino+oscillations+for+solar+neutrinos+observing+not+only+electron+neutrino+disappearance+but+also+active+neutrino+appearance..jpg
DESCRIPTION: None

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://image1.slideserve.com/3307367/sno-experiment-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A cross-sectional illustration of the SNO experiment at the Sudbury Mine in Canada depicts a spherical detector with a deuterium central ball surrounded by PMTs and water shield, with labeled features highlighting its components.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://image1.slideserve.com/1704223/recent-sno-results-for-solar-neutrinos-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A slide displays recent results from the Sudbury Neutrino Observatory (SNO), including measurements of solar neutrino flux, model predictions, and constraints on neutrino oscillations, accompanied by graphs illustrating flux parameter correlations and day/night flux differences.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image3.slideserve.com/5691726/the-sudbury-neutrino-observatory-experiment-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the Sudbury Neutrino Observatory experiment, showing the interactions of solar neutrinos with deuterons to produce protons, neutrons, and gamma rays, highlighting the detection processes for electron neutrinos and other neutrino flavors, with the goal of measuring neutrino and charged current rates.

enriched_points.expanded_content: The Sudbury Neutrino Observatory (SNO) measured solar neutrinos, confirming their oscillation and resolving the solar neutrino problem by observing multiple neutrino flavors.
Super-Kamiokande detected atmospheric neutrinos, demonstrating oscillations and supporting theories on neutrino mass, using a large water Cherenkov detector in Japan.
Daya Bay experiment studied antineutrinos from reactors, providing precise measurements of neutrino mixing angles and confirming the existence of neutrino masses.
The T2K experiment in Japan studies muon neutrino oscillations, sending neutrinos from an accelerator to a distant detector, enhancing our understanding of neutrino behavior.
NOvA explores neutrino oscillations by sending beams from Fermilab to detectors in Minnesota, aiming to measure differences in oscillation rates between muon and electron neutrinos.
